items.title: Matrix structure and notation
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  M["Matrix A (m x n)"] --> R["Rows (m)"]
  M --> C["Columns (n)"]
  R --> R1["Row 1"]
  R --> Rm["Row m"]
  C --> C1["Column 1"]
  C --> Cn["Column n"]
  R1 --> A11["a_11"]
  R1 --> A1n["a_1n"]
  Rm --> Am1["a_m1"]
  Rm --> Amn["a_mn"]
items.caption: A matrix A has m rows and n columns; each entry a_ij sits at the intersection of row i and column j.

items.title: Core matrix operations
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  A["A (m x n)"] --- B["B (m x n)"]
  A -- "+, - (entrywise)" --> Add["A + B, A - B"]
  S["Scalar c"] --> Scale["cA"]
  A --> Scale
  A --> T["A^T (n x m)"]
  note1["Addition/subtraction: same shape"]
  note2["Scalar multiplication: any shape"]
  note3["Transpose: swaps rows and columns"]
  Add -.-> note1
  Scale -.-> note2
  T -.-> note3
items.caption: Addition and subtraction require same shape, scalar multiplication works for any matrix, and transpose swaps rows and columns.
